name: next-auth
summary: Complete authentication for Next.js — OAuth providers (Google, GitHub, etc.), email magic links, JWT sessions, and database adapters in one package.

story.problem: Auth is complex to get right: OAuth flows, CSRF protection, session management, refresh tokens, secure cookies. Rolling your own is weeks of work and a security minefield.
story.mental_model: NextAuth exposes a catch-all API route (/api/auth/[...nextauth]) that handles all OAuth callbacks and session endpoints. Configure providers (Google, GitHub, credentials) and an adapter (database). Use useSession() in client components or getServerSession() in server components to access the authenticated user.
story.when_to_use: Any Next.js app needing auth — social login, email magic links, or username/password. It's the standard choice for Next.js auth.
story.when_not_to_use: Non-Next.js projects, when you need very complex RBAC that next-auth doesn't support natively, or when your team owns an existing auth service.

alternatives.name: clerk
alternatives.reason: Managed auth service — zero backend code, beautiful UI components

alternatives.name: lucia
alternatives.reason: Lightweight, framework-agnostic, you own the database

alternatives.name: supabase auth
alternatives.reason: If already using Supabase — built-in auth with row-level security

nodes.label: NextAuth()
nodes.summary: Creates the API route handler — configure providers, callbacks, adapter

nodes.label: useSession()
nodes.summary: Client-side hook — returns { data: session, status }

nodes.label: getServerSession()
nodes.summary: Server-side session access — use in server components, API routes

nodes.label: Database Adapter
nodes.summary: Persist users/sessions to DB — Prisma, Drizzle, Supabase adapters available

nodes.label: Callbacks
nodes.summary: jwt(), session(), signIn() — customize token/session shape and access control

tasks.title: Add Google + GitHub login to Next.js

steps.label: Create auth route
